title: Ready column shows empty on board
description:
Regression after UI-154 push-only board migration: some environments report an empty Ready column while other columns populate. Root cause appears to be missing/unsupported `bd ready` command, causing server to produce no membership for `ready-issues`.

Fix implemented in same PR:
- server/list-adapters.js: Add fallback in `fetchListForSubscription()` for `ready-issues`. If `bd ready` fails, compute Ready as Open minus Blocked using two list calls.

Acceptance criteria:
- Ready column populates in environments without `bd ready`.
- No RPC reads added back; stays push-only.
- Sorting and timeframe behavior unchanged.

title: bdui start prints/resolves home default db for Dolt workspace
description: With local .beads metadata (Dolt backend) and existing ~/.beads/default.db, bdui start still reports/resolves default.db. Update startup reporting/resolution path to use workspace-aware resolver.
notes:
Root cause: server/cli/daemon.printServerUrl() still used resolveDbPath(), so bdui start output could show ~/.beads/default.db in Dolt workspaces without local sqlite files. Switched it to resolveWorkspaceDatabase() and added server/cli/daemon.test.js regression coverage.
Reproduced: resolveDbPath() classifies ~/.beads/default.db as source=nearest via parent traversal under /Users/max, which causes incorrect workspace resolution in Dolt metadata-only workspaces.
Fixed root cause by changing resolveDbPath() to classify ~/.beads/default.db as source=home-default (fallback) instead of source=nearest when discovered via parent traversal. This prevents Dolt metadata-only workspaces under /Users/max from being misidentified as sqlite workspaces and stops runBd from forcing BEADS_DB to the global default. Removed unused findWorkspaceBeadsDb export and updated db tests with coverage for the home-default-vs-nearest edge case and metadata precedence.

title: Dolt backend still resolves to ~/.beads/default.db in local .beads projects
description: Starting server in a repo with .beads and Dolt backend still resolves DB path to ~/.beads/default.db instead of local project DB/discovery path. Reproduce from HEAD^ context and fix path resolution for Dolt backend.
notes: Implemented workspace-aware DB resolution for non-SQLite backends. Added resolveWorkspaceDatabase() in server/db.js and switched server startup, workspace registration/switching, and watcher binding to use it. Dolt workspaces now resolve to nearest .beads directory instead of ~/.beads/default.db fallback. Added regression tests in server/db.test.js and updated CLI command mocks in unit/integration tests.

title: Serialize bd commands for Dolt backend to avoid concurrent read crashes
description: Investigated empty issue list after Dolt migration. Reproduced that concurrent 'bd list --json' invocations crash with nil pointer panic in Dolt embedded mode. beads-ui can trigger this by issuing multiple list subscriptions in parallel. Add server-side serialization/mutex around bd invocations and regression tests.
close_reason: Fixed by serializing bd invocations in server/bd.js

comments.author: Maximilian Antoni
comments.text: Root cause reproduced: concurrent 'bd list --json' calls in Dolt embedded mode crash with nil pointer panic. beads-ui triggers parallel reads during multi-subscription startup. Fixed by serializing all runBd invocations in server/bd.js via a global promise queue so only one bd process runs at a time. Validation: npm run tsc, npm test, npm run lint, npm run prettier:write.

title: Prevent db watcher onChange loop from self-triggered reads
description: Watching DB file updates triggers UI subscription refresh and DB reads, which appear to update the DB file again, causing a 1s loop. Need workaround in server/watcher.js to avoid self-triggered updates.
close_reason: Added cooldown window after onChange to suppress self-triggered watcher loops; removed ineffective stat-based filter and added tests.

title: Show fatal bd stderr dialog when commands fail
description: During websocket subscribe, bd CLI exited with code 1 (args=[ 'list', '--json' ]) and stderr: 'Error: Database out of sync with JSONL. Run "bd sync --import-only" to fix.' The UI kept working and no dialog appeared. Fatal errors from bd should surface to the user with stderr content.
notes: Propagate subscribe-list bd errors back to the client and show stderr in a blocking dialog. Added fatal error dialog component and tests.

title: Details screen stuck on "Loading…" — error swallowed by try/catch
description: The details screen stopped working after the last `bd` update. The UI shows only "Loading…" and neither server nor browser logs show any errors. Likely an error is swallowed by try/catch. Add debug logging or rethrow errors so failures surface, and fix the underlying issue if small.
acceptance_criteria:
- Navigating to a details page renders the content, not a permanent "Loading…"
- If an error occurs fetching or rendering, a debug log is emitted to the console and/or server logs
- No try/catch blocks in the details flow swallow errors silently; errors are either logged or rethrown
- Basic manual test path works in Chrome and shows logs on failure
notes: Switching to fatal error dialog issue from latest logs; unassigning.

title: Status filter intermittently not applied on Issues screen
description:
User reports that changing the issue status filter on the Issues screen does not consistently update the list; sometimes the list remains in its previous state until another interaction occurs. Suspect state not derived from URL or missing effect dependency causing stale query parameters.

Steps observed by user: change status filter; list sometimes stays unchanged.

Goal: Ensure changing the status filter always triggers re-fetch/re-filter and the list reflects the selected status immediately.
acceptance_criteria:
- Changing status filter always updates the list immediately.
- Switching from a list with higher revision to a different status whose first snapshot has a lower revision still replaces the list (no stale data).
- Ready/Open/Closed/In Progress all behave consistently across repeated switches.
- Unit tests cover spec change with lower-revision snapshots.
notes:
Root cause: per-subscription issue store tracked a single last_revision per client id. After changing the subscription spec for 'tab:issues', a fresh snapshot with a lower revision could be ignored, leaving the old list visible. Fix: reset the store when the subscription spec key changes so revision counters start fresh for the new list.

Changes:
- app/data/subscription-issue-stores.js: replace store if spec key changes.
- Added test app/main.filter-switch-reset.test.js to assert accepting lower-revision snapshot after filter change.

Validation: `npm test`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run format:check` all pass locally.

title: Epics tab shows empty due to bd 'epic status' JSON shape (nested 'epic')
description:
When subscribing to the epics list, the server adapter mapped `epics` to `bd epic status --json` and then normalized array items assuming a top-level `id`. Current bd output nests the epic under an `epic` field, e.g. `{ epic: { id, title, ... }, total_children, closed_children }`. Our normalization discarded these entries (missing `id`), so the registry published an empty snapshot and the Epics view rendered no items.

Fix implemented:
- server/list-adapters.js: Special-case `epics` to flatten `stdoutJson` entries: lift `epic.id` and core fields to the top level, and preserve `total_children`, `closed_children`, `eligible_for_close`. Then pass through `normalizeIssueList`.
- app/views/epics.js: In `load()`, prefer numeric `total_children`/`closed_children` when present; fallback to deriving counts from `dependents` for seeded tests or legacy snapshots.

Validation:
- Ran `npm run typecheck`, `npm test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run format` — all green.
- WebSocket will now send a non-empty snapshot for `tab:epics` populated from the flattened data.

Notes:
- The view still subscribes to `issues-for-epic` with correct param `epic_id` for child rows.
- No changes to protocol envelopes.
acceptance_criteria:
- Epics subscription returns non-empty snapshot when bd returns nested `epic` shape
- Epics screen lists epics and shows progress bars immediately
- No regressions: typecheck/tests/lint/format all pass

title: Server rejects `subscribe-list` for `issue-detail`; remove `show-issue` RPC usage
description:
When opening issue details, the UI issues both `show-issue` and `subscribe-list` with `type: 'issue-detail'`. The server rejects the subscription with `bad_request: payload.type must be one of: all-issues, epics, issues-for-epic, blocked-issues, ready-issues, in-progress-issues, closed-issues`.

Hypothesis:
- Server validation enumerates allowed `subscribe-list` types and is missing `issue-detail`.
- Adding `issue-detail` support would make the `show-issue` read RPC redundant; the UI should render from push updates only.

Scope:
- Server: Allow `subscribe-list` with `type: 'issue-detail'` and params `{ id: string }`. Publish an initial snapshot + subsequent upserts/deletes for that issue via the existing push channel.
- UI: Remove `show-issue` RPC call and render the details view from the `issue-detail` subscription store. Ensure lifecycle unsubscribes on close/navigation.
- Tests: Add coverage for subscribe validation and details view data flow.
- Docs: Update protocol to mention `issue-detail` list type and deprecate `show-issue`.

Acceptance:
- A `subscribe-list` request with `type: 'issue-detail'` and `params: { id }` succeeds and streams the issue.
- No `show-issue` requests are sent from the UI.
- All unit tests pass; lint/typecheck/format pass.

title: Client: remove issues-changed; add detail subscription and store-driven rendering
description:
- Remove `client.on('issues-changed', ...)` and related coalesce logic from `app/main.js`.
- Subscribe to `detail:<id>` with `{ type: 'issue-detail', params: { id } }` when dialog opens; unsubscribe on close.

title: UI-151: Fix board/epics push-only regressions

title: Define push protocol v2 schema and handshake

notes: Doc added: docs/protocol/issues-push-v2.md. Includes envelope schema (topic/action/revision/snapshot/meta), payload types for added/updated/removed, subscribe handshake with version negotiation (accepts ["push-v2","push-v1"]) and reconnect behavior, ordering & batching with revision semantics, and migration notes for v1 fallback. title: Pure push-based issue updates (no fetch)
description: Move beads-ui to a pure push model for issue data. Today clients subscribe to server updates, but use those pushed notifications to trigger a fetch. Goal: Server pushes full issue JSON for `added` and `updated`, and only ids for `removed`. Initial subscribe returns an `added` snapshot with all issues. Client maintains a normalized local cache and re-renders directly from pushed data with no follow-up fetch.
design:
Push-only protocol (breaking):
- Message shape: { topic:"issues", action:"added"|"updated"|"removed", revision:number, snapshot?:boolean, payload:{ issues:Issue[] }|{ ids:string[] } }
- Handshake: Client subscribes with { subscribe:"issues" }; server replies { subscribed:"issues" }.
- Initial subscribe replies with a single `added` snapshot (all issues) and `snapshot:true`.
- Server batches multiple changes into arrays and increments `revision` monotonically per subscription.
- No version negotiation or v1 fallback; fetch-on-notify path removed.

Server:
- For `added`/`updated`, include full Issue JSON; for `removed`, only ids.
- Include `revision` on every message; batch when possible.

Client:
- Maintain normalized store keyed by id; reducers for `added`/`updated`/`removed`.
- Drive UI renders from store; remove issue-list fetch after subscribe.
- Resilience: tolerate out-of-order packets by ignoring stale `revision` (<= last applied).

Compatibility & Rollout:
- Breaking change. No feature flag, no phased rollout, no telemetry collection.
acceptance_criteria:
- After subscribe, there is no network fetch for the issue list in normal operation.
- Server emits full Issue JSON for `added`/`updated` and only ids for `removed`.
- Initial subscribe returns an `added` snapshot with all current issues.
- Client maintains a normalized cache and re-renders from pushes only.
- Unit + integration tests cover reducers and protocol behavior.
- Protocol doc reflects push-only.

title: Refactor data exchange to subscription-based updates
description: Overhaul the UI↔server data exchange by introducing list subscriptions, server-side diffing, and push-based updates, with robust watcher integration and mutation race control. See docs/data-exchange-subscription-plan.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- Clients can subscribe/unsubscribe to: all-issues, epics, issues-for-epic, blocked-issues, pending-issues, in-progress-issues, closed-issues.
- Server maintains per-subscription registry with itemsById (id→{updated_at, closed_at}) and subscriber set.

title: bdui: Daemonize server with PID management
description:
Implement background daemon management for the beads-ui server.

Scope
- Start: spawn `node server/index.js` as a detached child; redirect stdio to a log file; unref.
- PID file: write child PID to a runtime directory (prefer `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/beads-ui` or `os.tmpdir()/beads-ui`), ensure directory exists, and set safe permissions.
- Stop: read PID file, send SIGTERM, wait up to N seconds for clean exit, then SIGKILL as fallback; remove PID file.
- Restart: stop (ignore not-running) then start.
- Idempotency: `start` returns 0 if already running and prints the URL; guard against stale PID files.
- Port/host: respect `server/config.js` defaults and env overrides.
acceptance_criteria:
- `bdui start` leaves no child attached to the invoking terminal and returns 0.
- Log file written under the runtime dir and appended across runs.
- `bdui stop` terminates the daemon and removes the PID file; returns code 2 if not running.
- `bdui restart` performs stop then start and returns 0 on success.
- Stale PID file detection works (no crash), replacing the PID file when necessary.
- Works on macOS and Linux in local testing.
notes: Claiming UI-96. Implementing `bdui` CLI daemon management: start/stop/restart with PID + logs and idempotency. Will integrate as ESM binary per coding standards and respect server/config.js host/port.

title: Epic: Add `bdui` binary for UI daemon control
description:
Introduce a new CLI binary `bdui` to control the beads-ui server as a background daemon and streamline local usage.

Goals
- Provide `bdui` with commands: `start`, `stop`, `restart`.
- Spawn the UI server as a detached daemon (background process) with PID management.
- On successful `start`, auto-open the app in the OS default browser.
- Cross-platform support (macOS, Linux, Windows)
- Ship as a Node ESM binary, adhering to repo coding standards (JSDoc, ESM, lint/type/test).
- Update docs and add tests.

Notes
- Current server entry: `server/index.js` with Express + WS.
- Package currently `private`; for dev, support `npm link` to expose `bdui` locally.
- Prefer a PID file under a runtime dir (e.g. XDG runtime or OS tmp) to avoid repo writes; include an override via env or flag for debugging.
acceptance_criteria:
- Running `bdui --help` prints usage and lists `start|stop|restart`.
- `bdui start` ensures exactly one detached server instance, writes PID file, and prints the URL.
- `bdui start` opens the default browser to the server URL unless disabled with `--no-open` or `BDUI_NO_OPEN=1`.
- `bdui stop` terminates the daemon gracefully (SIGTERM) and removes the PID file.
- `bdui restart` behaves as `stop` then `start`.
- Exit codes: success=0; not-running on stop=2; invalid command=1.
- Works on macOS and Linux; Windows behavior documented and covered where feasible.
- CI passes: `npm run all` stays green.
notes: Docs updated: README.md and docs/quickstart.md now cover `bdui` usage, flags (`--no-open`), env (`BDUI_RUNTIME_DIR`, `BDUI_NO_OPEN`, `PORT`), and Windows notes. Tests added in UI-99 validate CLI parsing and daemon management. `npm run all` passes locally.

description:

Plan: Trace edit component → mutation call → store update. Fix payload/state handling and add a focused test.
Acceptance:
- Changing description updates server-side value.
- Editor keeps new text post-save.
- Reload shows updated description.
- No regressions in title/labels save flows.
notes:
Root cause: WebSocket server mapped `edit-text` to non-existent bd flags (`--description`, `--acceptance`). bd update actually expects `--notes` and `--acceptance-criteria`. This caused saves to error and UI to exit edit mode without persisting.
Fix: Updated server/ws.js to map description → `--notes` and acceptance → `--acceptance-criteria`. Added tests asserting correct flags in server/ws.mutations.test.js. Updated docs in app/protocol.md and docs/architecture.md. Ran typecheck, tests, lint, and prettier — all green.

title: Fix DB monitoring: watch SQLite DB instead of issues.jsonl
description:
The UI’s file watcher currently monitors `issues.jsonl` for changes. This is incorrect when using the SQLite backend; it should watch the resolved SQLite database file instead. As a result, UI updates are missed or stale when the DB changes.

Follow the same DB resolution order as beads CLI:
1) `--db` flag (e.g., `bd --db /path/to/db.db ...`)
2) `$BEADS_DB` environment variable
3) nearest `.beads/*.db` in current directory or ancestors (walk up like git)
4) `~/.beads/default.db` fallback

Notes:
- Re-resolve and reattach watchers when working directory or configuration changes.
- Handle the case where no DB is found with a clear, actionable message.
acceptance_criteria:
- Watches the resolved SQLite DB file instead of `issues.jsonl`.
- DB path resolution matches beads CLI precedence: `--db` > `$BEADS_DB` > nearest `.beads/*.db` (walk up) > `~/.beads/default.db`.
- Changing the active workspace or `--db` value rebinds the watcher without restart.
- When no DB can be resolved, surfaces a user-facing error with hints to set `--db`, `BEADS_DB`, or initialize with `bd init`.
- Removes any reliance on `issues.jsonl` for change detection in SQLite mode.
- Includes unit tests for resolution logic and watcher rebind behavior.
- Updates documentation to describe the new watching source and resolution order.
notes: Starting implementation: add db resolution per beads precedence (--db > BEADS_DB > nearest .beads/*.db > ~/.beads/default.db), refactor watcher to observe DB file, rebind on config changes, pass --db to bd runner, update tests and docs.

title: SPA web UI for bd (local app)
description:
Purpose: Build a local, single‑page web app to browse issues, change status, and edit text for the bd CLI tracker.

Scope and constraints:
- Backend: Express app in `/server` serving static files from `/app`.
- Realtime: All data exchange via WebSocket using the `ws` package.
- Live updates: Watch `.beads/issues.jsonl`; broadcast changes to the active page/clients.
- Frontend: `index.html` and ES modules in `/app` (vanilla JS, ESM, JSDoc types).
- Actions: Client editing sends WS messages; server maps them to `bd` commands.
- Testing: Use Vitest (Node + jsdom). Configure ESLint/Prettier and type checks via `tsc`.
- Standards: Follow project coding/testing standards provided by user instructions.

High‑level capabilities:
- List, filter, and sort issues.
- View issue details including dependencies.
- Update status and priority; edit title/description.
- Auto‑refresh on `.beads/issues.jsonl` changes; push‑based (no polling).
- Display ready work and basic stats.

Non‑goals (initial phase):
- Multi‑repo orchestration, auth, or remote access beyond localhost.
- Advanced visualization (graphs) beyond simple lists/trees.

WS message types (initial draft):
- `list-issues` { filters }
- `show-issue` { id }
- `update-status` { id, status }
- `edit-text` { id, field: 'title'|'description', value }
- `update-priority` { id, priority }
- `create-issue` { title, type, priority, description }
- `list-ready` {}
- `subscribe-updates` {}

bd command mapping (examples):
- list → `bd list --json [filters]`
- show → `bd show <id> --json`
- update status/priority → `bd update <id> --status ... | --priority ...`
- close → `bd close <id>`; reopen → `bd reopen <id>`
- create → `bd create "title" -t <type> -p <prio> -d "desc"`
- dependencies → `bd dep add|remove <a> <b>`; tree → `bd dep tree <id>` (optional)

Live update source:
- Observe `.beads/issues.jsonl` (JSON Lines). On change, server emits `issues-changed` with minimal payload (timestamp + touched ids if derivable, else invalidate caches).

Dev ergonomics:
- `npm run dev`: concurrently run Express+WS with watch (nodemon) and static app server.
- `npm test`: vitest unit tests.
- `npm run lint` and `npm run typecheck`.
acceptance_criteria:
- App lists issues from current bd DB and shows details.
- Status changes and text edits via UI reflect in bd DB and survive reload.
- `.beads/issues.jsonl` changes broadcast to connected clients; UI updates without manual refresh.
- All traffic uses a WebSocket; no fetch REST endpoints for data.
- Tests: At least core flows (list/show/update/edit) covered by Vitest.
- Repo contains `/server`, `/app`, and initial docs with run instructions.
notes: Duplicate of UI-1 (epic). The implemented app matches this scope: SPA under /app, server under /server, WebSocket transport, DB watcher broadcast, and tests/docs in place. Closing as completed by UI-1 and related tasks.

title: SPA web UI for bd (local, real-time)
description:
Build a local-first single-page web app for the `bd` (beads) CLI that lets users browse issues, change status, and edit text with real-time updates. The app talks to a local WebSocket server; the server watches `.beads/issues.jsonl` and executes `bd` commands corresponding to edits from the UI.

Scope
- Runs locally; no cloud dependencies
- SPA served from localhost; WebSocket for data
- Live updates driven by `.beads/issues.jsonl` watcher
- Editing in UI sends WS messages; server runs matching `bd` commands

Non-goals
- Multi-user concurrency beyond a single machine
- Authentication/authorization beyond localhost-only protections

Constraints
- ECMAScript modules; JSDoc types; `.ts` only for interface declarations
- Follow provided Coding and Testing Standards
- Prefer minimal dependencies; no heavy framework required
acceptance_criteria:
- User can view issues list and open an issue detail page.
- User can change issue status and edit text fields (title, description, acceptance) from the UI.
- UI changes propagate via WebSocket to a local server; server executes the corresponding `bd` command(s).
- File watcher on `.beads/issues.jsonl` pushes updates; the on-screen issue(s) update without refresh.
- Protocol documented (request/response + push events) with versioning and error model.
- Local-only server binding (127.0.0.1) with basic input validation.
- Tests cover protocol handlers and critical UI flows.
- Quickstart docs explain setup, run, and development workflow.
notes:
Implemented local-first SPA and server with live updates and bd integration.

Highlights
- UI: Issues list and detail views (`app/views/list.js`, `app/views/detail.js`) with status and priority controls plus inline title/description editing; navigation via hash router; app shell in `app/main.js`.
- Protocol: Documented v1.0.0 in `app/protocol.md` and shared symbols via `server/protocol.js`.
- WS transport: Robust client with reconnect and event dispatch (`app/ws.js`); server with request/response and push events, including heartbeat (`server/ws.js`).
- bd integration: Executes matching `bd` commands for list/show/update/edit/dep operations (`server/bd.js`, handlers in `server/ws.js`).
- Live updates: DB watcher broadcasts `issues-changed` and SPA refreshes list/detail accordingly (`server/watcher.js`, `app/main.js`).
- Local-only bind: Defaults to host `127.0.0.1` with configurable port (`server/config.js`).
- Tests: 59 passing tests across app + server covering protocol handlers and critical UI flows (`npm test`).
- Quickstart: Added `docs/quickstart.md` with setup, run, and workflow. DB watching details in `docs/db-watching.md`.

Notes
- The implementation watches the active beads SQLite DB (current beads store) rather than a historical `issues.jsonl`. The doc was updated to reflect this.
- The UI currently supports editing title and description. If acceptance text editing is required, propose a small follow-up to extend the `edit-text` handler and UI.

title: Speed up issue detail initial render
description:
Purpose:
Speed up perceived issue detail loading after board/list issue clicks.

Scope:
Client-side detail initial render and comment loading guard.

Inputs:
Live measurement showed board snapshot in ~5 ms, issue-detail snapshot in ~1.0s, and get-comments in ~1.7s for a zero-comment issue.

Allowed changes:
Frontend detail/store code, focused tests, rebuilt frontend bundle.

Do not touch:
3002 deployment, unrelated visual changes, CHANGES.md.

Expected output:
Issue details render immediately from already-loaded list data and still enrich from server detail snapshot.

Validation required:
Focused tests, full test suite, lint, typecheck, prettier check, rebuilt bundle.

Escalation triggers:
Incorrect detail data, skipped real detail snapshot, stale comments after updates.

Handoff format:
Summary with files, validation, and restart note.
design: Seed detail subscription stores from active tab stores without advancing server revision; preserve comments metadata across detail snapshots that omit it; keep server issue-detail subscription as source of truth.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking an issue can render detail content from active list data before bd show returns, zero-comment issues do not call get-comments, and existing detail enrichment still works.
notes: Measured kvm-foo-vsm3 before fix: board snapshot 5 ms, detail snapshot 1010 ms, get-comments 1672 ms.

title: View switching shows stuck Loading indicator
description:
## Problem
When switching between views (Issues → Board → Epics), the Loading indicator in the header often stays visible indefinitely, even after data has loaded or when there's no data to load.

## Observed Behavior
- Loading spinner appears immediately on view switch (correct)
- Spinner often doesn't clear after subscriptions complete
- Sometimes clears after 2+ seconds, sometimes never clears
- Affects both workspace switching and view switching
- Same behavior on both `main` and `feature/multi-project-workspace` branches

## Technical Context
The Loading indicator is managed by `app/utils/activity-indicator.js`:
- Uses a `pending_count` that increments on request start and decrements on completion
- The `wrapSend()` function wraps WebSocket sends to track activity
- If a request hangs or doesn't properly complete, the counter never decrements

Board view subscribes to 4 separate lists:
- `tab:board:blocked` (blocked-issues)
- `tab:board:ready` (ready-issues)  
- `tab:board:in-progress` (in-progress-issues)
- `tab:board:closed` (closed-issues)

Each spawns a separate `bd` command via `fetchListForSubscription()`.

## Potential Causes
1. Race condition in subscription acknowledgment
2. WebSocket message handling not calling `done()` in all code paths
3. Error responses not properly decrementing the counter
4. Subscription refresh not properly tracked by activity indicator

## Files to Investigate
- `app/utils/activity-indicator.js` - Activity tracking logic
- `app/main.js` - `ensureTabSubscriptions()` and `clearAndResubscribe()`
- `server/ws.js` - Subscription handling and response sending
- `app/data/subscriptions.js` - Client-side subscription management

## Reproduction Steps
1. Start bdui server: `./bin/bdui.js start`
2. Open http://127.0.0.1:3000/#/issues
3. Click on "Board" tab
4. Observe Loading indicator - it may stay visible indefinitely
5. Try switching workspaces and views multiple times
close_reason: Fixed loading indicator stuck issue by: 1) Fixed server-side lock race condition in withKeyLock(), 2) Added client-side pending_subscriptions tracking to prevent duplicates, 3) Added 30-second safety timeout to activity indicator

title: Bug: duplicate 'subscribe-list' for 'tab:issues' on issue click
description:
Clicking an issue in the Issues view triggers two additional `subscribe-list` calls for `tab:issues` (e.g., `type: 'all-issues'`).

Root cause: `app/main.js` subscribed to `tab:issues` in both `ensureTabSubscriptions()` (called from `onRouteChange`) and a separate `store.subscribe` block for filter changes, so any state change (like `selected_id`) caused two subscribe requests.

Fix:
- Remove the redundant filter-change subscription block.
- Add memoized spec comparison in `ensureTabSubscriptions()` to only resubscribe when the subscription spec changes (e.g., filters -> status segment).

Validation:
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npm test` passes (62 files, 210 tests).
- `npm run lint` and `npm run format:check` pass.

Acceptance:
- Clicking an issue from the Issues list does not emit duplicate `subscribe-list` calls for `tab:issues`.
- Changing filters emits at most one `subscribe-list` for `tab:issues`, only when the effective spec changes.
- No regressions in list/detail rendering or push updates.
notes: Implemented fix in app/main.js: removed redundant store.subscribe block that re-subscribed tab:issues on any state change; added memoized spec key in ensureTabSubscriptions() to resubscribe only when the effective list spec changes. Ran full validation (typecheck, tests, lint, format).

title: Replace unsupported ‘issues-for-epic’ subscription with epic detail + dependents
description:
Expanding an epic currently sends a WebSocket subscribe-list with type `issues-for-epic` which maps to `bd list --json --epic <id>`. The `bd` CLI does not support `--epic`, so this is incorrect. Refactor the protocol, server, and app to remove `issues-for-epic` entirely and load epic children via the epic’s detail subscription (`issue-detail`) using its `dependents` list.

Scope:
- Remove `issues-for-epic` from protocol docs, server validators, list-adapters, and tests.
- Epics UI: when expanding an epic, subscribe to `issue-detail` for the epic id and render children from `detail:<id>` snapshot’s `dependents` array. No per-epic list subscription.
- Update selectors to derive epic children from the `detail:<id>` store and sort consistently.
- Adjust unit tests accordingly.
- Keep existing `epics` (status) list subscription intact.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Expanding an epic triggers `subscribe-list` with `{ type: 'issue-detail', params: { id: '<epic-id>' } }` and registers `detail:<id>` store.
- Children rows in Epics view come from the epic detail’s `dependents` and exclude closed items.
- Server rejects `issues-for-epic` as an unknown subscription type.
- All server and app tests pass; docs mention no `issues-for-epic`.
notes:
Implemented removal of `issues-for-epic` and refactor to epic detail + dependents.

Changes:
- Server: removed `issues-for-epic` mapping and validation (server/list-adapters.js, server/validators.js), updated tests (server/list-adapters.test.js, server/ws.list-subscriptions.test.js).
- App: Epics view now subscribes to `issue-detail` on expand and renders children from `detail:<id>` snapshot’s `dependents` (app/views/epics.js). Updated selectors to read from detail store (app/data/list-selectors.js) and tests (app/views/epics.test.js, app/data/list-selectors.test.js).
- Types: extended Issue with `dependencies`/`dependents` and relaxed timestamp fields (types/subscriptions.ts).
- Docs: removed/updated references to `issues-for-epic` (docs/data-exchange-subscription-plan.md, docs/adr/001-push-only-lists.md).

Validation:

- npm run typecheck: OK.
- npm run lint/format: OK.

title: CLI: `bdui restart` always opens browser; should only open with `--open`
description:
Problem
- Running `bdui restart` launches the default browser unconditionally because `handleRestart()` calls `handleStart()` without options, and `handleStart()` opens by default when options are omitted.

Fix
- Make `handleStart()` default to not opening the browser unless explicitly requested.
- Pass through options from `restart` so `--open` works for restart as well.
- Update CLI argument handling for `restart` to mirror `start` (`--open`, `BDUI_NO_OPEN=1`).
- Adjust usage text to reflect start/restart.

Testing
- Add CLI tests asserting `restart --open` propagates the flag.

title: Markdown: support ordered lists using `1)` marker (CommonMark)
description:
The Markdown renderer should recognize ordered list markers using a right parenthesis, per CommonMark. Currently only `1.` is parsed as a list; `1)` renders as plain text.

Context
- CommonMark allows both `1.` and `1)` as ordered list markers.
- Users expect `1)` to start a list just like `1.`.

Change
- Update the Markdown parsing to treat `N)` at the start of a line as an ordered list marker, with the same semantics as `N.`.
- Ensure numbering behavior matches CommonMark (auto-increment when using repeated `1)` etc.).

Notes
- Verify no regressions for code blocks, fenced code, or inline text like "Version 1) beta" that should not start a list.
- If this is a parser configuration gap, prefer configuration over custom hacks.
acceptance_criteria:
- Input starting with `1) ` on a new line begins an ordered list per CommonMark.
- `1) One\n2) Two\n3) Three` renders as an ordered list 1–3.
- `1) One\n1) Two\n1) Three` renders as 1, 2, 3 (auto numbering) if supported by the parser.
- Inline text like `Not a list 1) example` does not start a list.
- Nested lists using `)` markers work: `1) Outer\n   1) Inner` renders correctly.
- Unit tests cover the above cases.
notes: Claimed by agent on 2025-10-25. Will update Markdown parsing to recognize `N)` list markers and add tests per acceptance criteria.

title: Events: coalesce issues-changed to avoid redundant full refresh
description:
Problem
- Editing an issue triggers two UI refreshes:
  (a) an `issues-changed` event with specific IDs, and then
  (b) a file-watcher-driven `issues-changed` without IDs after the DB file write,
  which causes a full cache refresh.

Impact
- Extra work and flicker; unnecessary network/CPU and slower UX.

Proposal
- Introduce an event coalescer in the UI data layer to merge near-simultaneous events.
- Prefer ID-scoped updates when available and suppress trailing full refreshes that cover the same change window.

Design Sketch
1) Event types
   - `issues-changed:{ids:Set<string>, token?:string, source:'local'|'watcher'|'remote'}`
   - `db-updated:{token?:string}` from the file watcher path (no ids).
2) Coalescing
   - Maintain a sliding window (e.g., 100–200 ms) queue. If an ID-scoped event is received, record its `token` (if any) and changed IDs.
   - If a `db-updated` arrives within the window and its `token` matches or an ID-scoped event was seen, drop or downgrade the full refresh.
   - Otherwise, perform a single full refresh after the window elapses.
3) Source suppression
   - Tag locally-initiated saves with a `token` propagated through to the write and back from the watcher so the UI can distinguish self-originated writes.
4) Safety valve
   - If any error occurs or the ID set is too large, fall back to a full refresh.

Testing
- Unit tests for: only-id updates; watcher-only updates; both within window; mismatched tokens; large ID set fallback.
- Add lightweight logging behind debug flag to verify coalescing in dev.
design:
Server-side authoritative diff emission with optional optimistic UI event.

Overview
- Maintain an in-memory index Map<issue_id, digest> on the server.
- On DB watcher event, debounce 100–200 ms to let writes settle.
- Load current issues, build `next` index using a canonical projection and stable stringify.
- Compute `added`, `removed`, `updated` by comparing `prev` vs `next` (digest via fast non-crypto hash, e.g., xxhash64).
- Emit a single post-commit event: `issues-changed:{ added: string[], updated: string[], removed: string[], rev: string|number, source: 'watcher'|'local'|'remote', token?: string }`.
- Update `prev = next` and bump `rev` (monotonic counter or file ETag/mtime+size).

Local writes
- Option A (simplest): Do not emit immediate UI events on local writes; rely solely on the post-commit diff event.
- Option B (optimistic): Emit immediate `issues-changed:{ids}` with `token`; when the server diff arrives, include the same `token` so the UI can suppress duplicate work.

Safety & fallbacks
- If parsing fails, hashing fails, or the change set exceeds a configured threshold, emit a single `issues-changed:{}` without IDs to signal a full refresh.
- Prefer atomic writes (temp + rename) so the watcher observes finalized state.

A11y/UX impact
- UI still supports coalescing as a thin guard, but should rarely need it once server emits precise diffs.

Scaling notes
- If DB grows large, prefer per-issue `updated_at`/`revision` fields or a server-side change journal to avoid O(N) hashing on every write.
acceptance_criteria:
- A single edit results in exactly one server-emitted `issues-changed` event per DB commit.
- When the edit affects specific issues, the event includes precise `added`, `updated`, and `removed` arrays; the UI applies only incremental updates, with no subsequent full refresh.
- Local writes: either (A) only the server diff is delivered, or (B) an optimistic `{ids, token}` is followed by the server diff that the UI suppresses based on matching `token` or increasing `rev`.
- External/process edits (no token) still produce precise `added/updated/removed` sets.
- Deletions are explicitly included in `removed`, and the UI evicts those issues from cache.
- Rapid consecutive writes coalesce via debounce into one event, and `rev` increases monotonically.
- On parser/read errors or oversized change sets, the server emits a single `issues-changed` without IDs; the UI performs exactly one full refresh.
- Unit tests cover: only-id updates, watcher-only updates, optimistic + server diff, mismatched tokens, large change fallback, and multiple rapid writes.
- Debug logging behind a flag verifies coalescing and emitted payloads during development.
notes:
Implemented UI-side coalescing for `issues-changed` events to suppress redundant full refreshes following targeted (ID-scoped) updates.

Changes
- app/main.js: Added a short suppression window (200 ms) after receiving an ID-scoped `issues-changed`; a trailing watcher-only event within that window is ignored. This preserves immediate responsiveness while avoiding duplicate reloads and flicker.
- app/main.live-updates.coalesce.test.js: New tests covering coalescing for list and detail views.

Validation
- npm run typecheck: OK
- npm test: 171 tests passing (including new coalescing tests)
- npm run lint / format: OK

Notes
- This UI-side coalescer is a thin guard aligned with the proposed server-side diffing plan. When server emits precise diffs in the future, the UI logic will remain compatible and simply reduce to fewer suppressions.

title: Board: focusable cards + keyboard navigation + focus restore after dialog close
description:
Make Kanban board cards fully keyboard accessible and add intuitive arrow key navigation. Restore card focus after closing the issue detail dialog.

Context
- Current board is hard to navigate using the keyboard and does not consistently restore focus from dialogs.

Details
- Make each card a focusable element with visible focus ring.
- Up/Down: move focus to the previous/next card within the same column.
- Left/Right: move focus to the top card of the previous/next column.
- Enter/Space: open the issue detail dialog for the focused card.
- Escape: closes dialog. On close, focus returns to the previously focused card.
- Use ARIA roles (`list`, `listitem`, or `grid` pattern) and `aria-label`/`aria-describedby` for card information.
- If a column has no cards, skip it when using Left/Right.

Notes
- Use roving `tabindex` in columns to keep a single tabbable element where appropriate.
- Preserve native arrow behavior when inside editable controls within a card.
acceptance_criteria:
- Tabbing places focus on a card; focus style is clearly visible.
- Up/Down moves between cards within the same column; Left/Right jumps to the top card in the adjacent column.
- Columns with zero cards are skipped when navigating with Left/Right.
- Enter opens the card’s detail dialog; Escape closes it and returns focus to the same card as before opening.
- Screen reader announces card context (issue title and column name).
- Unit tests cover navigation and focus restoration; manual accessibility check passes with VoiceOver/NVDA.
notes:
Implementation summary (2025-10-24):
- Cards are now focusable with a visible focus ring. Implemented roving tabindex per column so only one card is tabbable at a time; arrow keys update the roving index.
- Keyboard navigation:
  - ArrowUp/ArrowDown move between cards within the same column.
  - ArrowLeft/ArrowRight jump to the top card of the previous/next non-empty column (columns with zero cards are skipped).
  - Enter/Space open the issue detail dialog for the focused card.
- A11y roles + labels:
  - Column bodies use role="list" and reference their headers via aria-labelledby.
  - Cards use role="listitem" and get an aria-label combining title and column name.
- Focus restoration after dialog close:
  - Dialog captures the previously focused element when opening and restores focus to it on close (button, Esc, or programmatic close).
- Tests added:
  - app/views/board.navigation.test.js covers ArrowUp/Down and Left/Right (with empty column skip) and Enter/Space activation.
  - app/views/issue-dialog.focus.test.js verifies focus restoration to the previously focused element.
- Styles: .board-card:focus gets an accessible outline.

Files changed:
- app/views/board.js (a11y + keyboard nav)
- app/views/issue-dialog.js (focus restore)
- app/styles.css (focus outline)
- app/views/board.navigation.test.js (new)
- app/views/issue-dialog.focus.test.js (new)

title: Add support for issue labels in UI
